data.title: OnKure started at outperform by Oppenheimer, OKI-219 potential cited
data.description:
• Oppenheimer started coverage of OnKure Therapeutics (NASDAQ:OKUR) with an outperform rating, citing the potential of its breast cancer drug candidate OKI-219.
  • The investment bank said it believes OKI-219 could become the “go-to option for one of the most frequent mutations in breast cancer” due in part to its selectivity as a PI3Ka inhibitor.
  • Oppenheimer said while the drug would enter a crowded market, it believes OKI-219’s high selectivity will make it more tolerable and, hence, more easily combinable with other therapies.
  • “In a world where triplet or even quadruplet therapies could be the norm, we think combinability will be king,” the bank added.
  • Oppenheimer set its price target for the stock at $35.

data.title: Akebia gains additional CMS reimbursement for end-stage renal disease drug Vafseo
data.description:
• CMS has determined that Akebia Therapeutics' (NASDAQ:AKBA) Vafseo (vadadustat) meets criteria for the Transitional Drug Add-On Payment Adjustment (TDAPA) for anemia management of end-stage renal disease starting on Jan. 1, 2025.
  • TDAPA status, part of the prospective payment system functional category, provides for two years of reimbursement for Vafseo plus the ESRD bundled rate to dialysis organizations.
  • TDAPA is designed to provide newer drugs to Medicare patients by proving additional payments to absorb the higher costs.
  • Also, Akebia was given a Level II Healthcare Common Procedure Coding System (HCPCS) code for Vafseo for billing by dialysis organizations for Medicare enrollees.

data.title: Castor Maritime announces delivery of the M/V Magic Ariel
data.description:
• Castor Maritime (NASDAQ:CTRM) has announced that on Oct. 9, 2024, it took delivery of the M/V Magic Ariel, the 2020-built Kamsarmax bulk carrier vessel it had agreed to acquire as previously announced on Sept. 30, 2024.
  • The vessel acquisition was financed in its entirety with cash on hand.
  • The M/V Magic Ariel will be employed under a time charter contract with a minimum duration of about seven months at a gross daily rate equal to 108% of the Baltic Panamax Index 5TC (BPI5TC).

data.title: Atlanta Fed's Bostic is 'definitely open' to skipping rate cut in November - report
data.description:
Federal Reserve Bank of Atlanta President Raphael Bostic said Thursday he was "definitely open" to holding interest rates steady at the central bank's November gathering, as disinflation appeared to stall with the latest CPI data.

Earlier on Thursday, the U.S. Bureau of Labor Statistics released a fresh read on the closely-watched Consumer Price Index, which rose more than expected M/M in September, signaling inflation risks remain. That's on top of the surprisingly strong September jobs report.

“I think we have the ability to be patient and wait and let things play out a little longer…. There are elements of today’s [CPI] report which I think validate that view,” Bostic said in an interview with the Wall Street Journal.

Calling the CPI report "janky," Bostic said he is "open to not moving at one of the last two meeting [this year] if the data comes in as I expect," he added. Bostic, who has penciled in a total of 75 basis points worth of easing this year, has long anticipated a bumpy disinflation trend.

It was only at the end of last month when Bostic said he'd be willing to support another 50-bp cut at the November meeting.

Then again, a case could be made to cut rates at either or both remaining meetings, he said. "It's a journey to get to neutral and the nuance of, 'Do you move 25 basis points here? 50 basis points there?' I don't think those are as consequential."

data.title: Tronox, LSB Industries upgraded at UBS on construction recovery
data.description:
Tronox (NYSE:TROX) on Thursday was upgraded to Buy from a previous investment rating of Neutral by analysts at financial-services firm UBS. They also upgraded LSB Industries (NYSE:LXU) to Buy from Neutral.

Shares of LSB (LXU) jumped as much as 16% following the upgrade.

Tronox upgrade

“Our updated 2025/26 EBITDA estimates are 9%/8% above consensus. We believe Tronox (TROX) EBITDA over the last 2 years has been hindered by lower op rates and some one-time issues as production restarts," Joshua Spector, analyst at UBS, said in an October 10 report.

UBS raised its price target on Tronox (TROX) to $19 a share from $17 a share, based on an estimated enterprise value that’s 6.5 times earnings before interest, taxes, depreciation and amortization.

LSB Industries upgrade

“Normal seasonality and 3Q downtime mask an earnings improvement that will become visible in 2025,” Spector said in an October 10 report. “We also see the ammonia/derivatives markets having early success in reduced carbon premiums, which should benefit LSB's (LXU) earnings into 2026 and would be upside to our estimates.”

UBS raised its price target on LSB (LXU) to $11 a share from $9.50 a share previously, based on an estimated enterprise value that’s 6.5 times earnings before interest, taxes, depreciation and amortization.

data.title: S&P, Dow, Nasdaq fluctuate as CPI warms, jobless claims come in hotter than expected
data.description:
Wall Street's major equity averages swayed between small gains and losses Thursday, after September consumer inflation and weekly jobless claims surpassed estimates, data that will feed into the Federal Reserve's thinking on interest rates.

A day after each closed at record highs, the S&P 500 (SP500) -0.2%, and the Dow Jones Industrial Average (DJI) -0.2% in early afternoon trade. The Nasdaq Composite (COMP:IND) was off less than -0.1%.

Eight of the S&P 500's 11 sectors fell, led by Real Estate. Energy topped advancers, with oil futures (CL1:COM) (CO1:COM) up more than +3%, as Hurricane Milton tore through parts of western Florida, and traders continued to wait for Israel’s possible military response to Iran’s recent missile attack.

The latest economic data arrived after scorching growth in September payrolls and the Federal Reserve's big half-point rate cut at its meeting last month as it considered cooling trends in inflation and slowing in the labor market.

Investors on Thursday saw inflation rates rose in September, with the Bureau of Labor Statistics posting +3.3% Y/Y growth in core CPI vs. +3.2% expected and +3.2% in August. Shelter, and motor vehicle insurance were among the upside price drivers. See a reaction roundup to the CPI report.

Meanwhile, initial jobless claims for the week ended Oct. 5 rose by 33K to 258K, vs. 231K consensus, a week when the U.S. contended with hurricanes and labor strikes.

"The CPI print [suggests] that inflation inched slightly higher while initial unemployment claims ticked similarly edged higher -- not the combination the Fed wants to see continue," Quincy Krosby, chief global strategist at LPL Financial, said in a note.

"If inflation data continues to indicate that prices are generally rising amid a backdrop of a cooler labor market, the Fed's next meeting will undoubtedly involve a more heated discussion of which of the Fed's mandates takes precedence," she said, adding that CPI report may spur concerns that a "mild form of stagflation" is simmering.

In the bond market, the 10-year Treasury yield (US10Y) was up 4 basis points to 4.11% as prices fell, while the 2-year yield (US2Y) was down 3 basis points to 3.98%.

Major U.S. stock averages rose Wednesday, after the Federal Reserve's September meeting minutes said a "substantial majority" on its rate-setting board backed its big half-point cut to start an easing cycle.

On Thursday, Federal Reserve Bank of New York President John Williams said he expects policymakers to make more rate cuts as the central bank makes progress in reducing inflation.

“Based on my current forecast for the economy, I expect that it will be appropriate to continue the process of moving the stance of monetary policy to a more neutral setting over time,” Williams said in remarks for an appearance at Binghamton University.

data.title: CPI read: Airfares move higher in September to make it four months out of five
data.description:
Airfares in the U.S. were up 1.6% in September compared to a year ago on an unadjusted basis. Overall, core inflation was up 3.3% year-over-year during the month, which was up from the +3.2% pace in August.

On a month-to-month comparison, airfares were up 3.2% in September after rising 3.9% in August, according to data compiled by the Bureau of Transportation Statistics. Airfares have now increased in four of the last five months. Looking ahead, Delta Air Lines (DAL) warned that the U.S. election could lead to a pause in demand. "We do anticipate seeing a little choppiness around the election, which we’ve seen in past national elections," stated CEO Ed Bastian in an interview. "Consumers will, I think, take a little bit of pause in making investment decisions, whether it's discretionary or other things. I think you’re going to hear other industries talking about that as well," he added. Analysts think holiday demand will be strong, with the short time period between the Thanksgiving Day holiday and Christmas holiday seen as supportive of pricing.

Airline stocks: American Airlines (AAL), Delta Air Lines (DAL), Southwest Airlines (LUV), United Airlines (UAL), JetBlue Airways (JBLU), Alaska Air Group (ALK), Allegiant Travel (ALGT), Spirit Airlines (SAVE), Mesa Airlines (MESA), SkyWest (SKYW), Sun Country Airlines (SNCY), and Frontier Group (ULCC).

data.title: Alvotech application for Prolia/Xgeva biosimilar accepted by EMA
data.description:
• Alvotech (NASDAQ:ALVO) said the European Medicines Agency has accepted its application to market its drug AVT03 as a biosimilar to Amgen’s (AMGN) Prolia and Xgeva.
  • The Icelandic drug developer plans to manufacture AVTO3. STADA Arzneimittel AG and Dr. Reddy’s Laboratories (RDY) have entered into semi-exclusive agreements with Alvotech (ALVO) to commercialize the product in Europe, including Switzerland and the U.K., according to a statement.
  • Both Prolia and Xgeva are based on the monoclonal antibody denosumab. Amgen (AMGN) markets the therapy as Prolia for the treatment of osteoporosis and Xgeva for the treatment of certain bone cancers and the prevention of bone complications caused by cancer that has spread to the bones.
  • Alvotech (ALVO) said the European market for denosumab is currently valued at around $1B.

data.title: Long-term mortgage rates see largest weekly rise in about 6 months
data.description:
Long-term mortgage rates saw the largest one-week rise in nearly six months, the Freddie Mac Primary Mortgage Survey said.

30-year fixed-rate mortgages averaged 6.32% as of Oct. 10, higher than 6.12% last week but down from 7.57% in the year-ago week.

15-year fixed-rate mortgages averaged 5.41%, compared to 5.25% in the prior week and 6.89% a year ago.

"Following the release of a stronger-than-expected September jobs report, the 30-year fixed rate mortgage saw the largest one-week increase since April," said Sam Khater, Freddie Mac's chief economist.

"However, we should remember that the rise in rates is largely due to shifts in expectations and not the underlying economy, which has been strong for most of the year," added Khater.

data.title: Single stock options liquidity is still extremely elevated for mega-caps vs. rest of market
data.description:
Daily volumes for options in the S&P 500 Index (SPX), the S&P 500 Trust (SPY), the Nasdaq 100 Index (NDX), and the Invesco QQQ Trust (QQQ) are significantly higher than any single stock.

But the notional value traded in the top single stocks – (NVDA), (TSLA), (AAPL), (META), (MSFT) – continues to be highly more elevated than most stocks.

Nvidia (NVDA), Tesla (TSLA), Apple (AAPL) and Meta Platforms (META) options are all more liquid than Russell 2000 Index (RTY) options, with $16.4M in three-month average notional volume. The former four have $49.7M, $41.4M, $20.4M, and $18.6M respectively.

Options positioning for the mega-cap tech stocks with the highest three-month average daily notional trading volume “represent a large percentage of risk traded on a daily basis,” said John Marshall, head of derivatives research at Goldman Sachs, in a note.
